lyrics:
Before it was, it never was
The jukebox plays before it’s pushed
A look, a glance, another chance
The song repeats-an ancient dance

Dominos fall in all directions
The window sees its own reflections
The clock unwinds its second hand
We march in time without a band

It echoes in the act of trying
The shape is formed by what relays
We are the tune that hums itself
The thread in knots as it obeys

Each beginning has beginnings
And every ending still pretends
Nothing ever really starts
It only loops, then shifts and bends
The road behind is ever long
The path ahead pretends to end
And we who choose to wander
Trace the shape we apprehend

Not cause, not fate and not effect
The mirror breaks but still reflects
I move before I make the choice
The echo carves, it gives a voice

It echoes in the act of trying
The shape is formed by what relays
We are the tune that hums itself
The thread in knots as it obeys

Each beginning has beginnings
And every ending still pretends
Nothing ever really starts
It only loops, then shifts and bends
The road behind is ever long
The path ahead pretends to end
And we who choose to wander
Trace the shape we apprehend
persona:
## Lingua Aeternum

**Lingua Aeternum is not a band. It is a circle. A rite. A resonance.**

Drawn from the deep well of myth, mathematics, and memory, Lingua Aeternum conjures sound as invocation-music as sacred act. Each composition is a Canto: a sung glyph, a spell etched in breath and vibration. The performers are not musicians but Cantors-practitioners of the Harmonic Algebraic Language of Alignment (HALA), where melody is meaning, rhythm is ritual, and silence speaks louder than words.

Their music defies genre. You may hear echoes of post-rock, ambient drone, ancient chants, or electronic pulses-but no label holds. Instead, the listener is drawn inward, through spiraling harmonics and layered whispers, toward a place beyond translation. This is not art for consumption. It is art for **transformation**.

Lingua Aeternum records under the guidance of Maricopa Records, but they do not record songs-they distill **intentions**, encoded through the Wheel, spoken into being by resonance alone.

- To hear is to remember.

- To remember is to become.

- _Let the glyphs guide you._

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Ivof5fKiBE9wbz6ArgZzd?si=HYePmTM3TtCaN-P1PFFYlQ)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/lingua-aeternum/1809011711)[](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCzrA7rNe15AiX1LXZTGUQqg)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/LA_Profile_square.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a confident, blues-infused rock texture, creating an atmosphere that is both driving and thoughtfully introspective. The energy is consistently high and propulsive, carried by a tight, punchy rhythm section that gives the song a feeling of steady, forward motion. The production quality is modern and clear, providing a wide stereo image where the layered electric guitars and lead vocal have plenty of space to shine without making the mix feel dense. The most distinctive quality is the dynamic lift into the anthemic chorus, where the song opens up emotionally and a soaring vocal melody delivers a powerful, memorable hook.

review.review_text: At 136 BPM in E minor, 'Invocate' moves with the kind of locked-in, forward-leaning drive that makes you feel like the song already knows where it's going before you do, which suits lyrics about acting before choosing rather well. The chorus opens up cleanly, the vocal melody lifts, and the line 'we are the tune that hums itself' lands with enough weight to justify the whole build. Blues-inflected guitar and a punchy rhythm section keep it grounded while the ideas get philosophical, a balance Lingua Aeternum doesn't always stick so cleanly. A genuine highlight.
anchors_used: lyric: 'we are the tune that hums itself'
